name: Hagia Sophia
description: Historic mosque and former cathedral with magnificent Byzantine architecture, mosaics, and one of the world's largest domes.

name: Topkapi Palace
description: Ottoman imperial palace museum housing priceless treasures, the Harem, and relics of the Prophet Muhammad.

name: Blue Mosque (Sultanahmet)
description: Iconic 17th-century Ottoman mosque famous for its six minarets and stunning blue Iznik tile interior.

name: Grand Bazaar
description: One of the world's oldest and largest covered markets with over 4,000 shops selling jewellery, spices, carpets, and more.

name: Bosphorus Strait Cruise
description: Scenic boat tour along the Bosphorus Strait passing Dolmabahce Palace, Rumeli Fortress, and the bridge between Europe and Asia.

name: Dolmabahce Palace
description: Opulent 19th-century Ottoman palace on the Bosphorus shore, featuring a crystal chandelier gifted by Queen Victoria.

name: Basilica Cistern
description: Atmospheric 6th-century underground cistern with 336 marble columns, eerie lighting, and the famous Medusa head bases.

name: Galata Tower
description: Medieval Genoese stone tower rising 67 metres above Beyoglu, offering sweeping 360-degree views of the Golden Horn and Bosphorus.

name: Suleymaniye Mosque
description: Masterpiece of architect Sinan crowning the Third Hill, with serene gardens, tombs of Suleiman the Magnificent, and Golden Horn views.

name: Istanbul Modern Art Museum
description: Turkey's leading contemporary art museum in a striking Renzo Piano waterfront building, showcasing Turkish and international works.

name: Spice Bazaar (Egyptian Bazaar)
description: Vibrant 17th-century market overflowing with colourful spices, Turkish delight, dried fruits, and aromatic teas beside the Golden Horn.

name: Kadikoy Market & Food Tour
description: Bustling Asian-side neighbourhood market where locals shop for fresh produce, street food, meze, and artisan goods along lively lanes.

name: Princes' Islands Ferry Trip
description: Relaxing ferry ride to the car-free Princes' Islands for horse-drawn carriage rides, Victorian mansions, and pine-shaded beaches.

name: Goreme Open Air Museum
description: UNESCO World Heritage cave churches and monasteries carved into volcanic rock, with remarkable Byzantine frescoes.

name: Cappadocia Hot Air Balloon
description: Iconic sunrise balloon ride over fairy chimneys, rose valleys, and the surreal Cappadocian landscape.

name: Derinkuyu Underground City
description: Ancient multi-level underground city reaching 85 m depth, once sheltering 20,000 people from invaders.

name: Red Valley Hiking Trail
description: Scenic trekking through the Red and Rose Valleys past cave churches, vineyards, and panoramic fairy chimney viewpoints.

name: Love Valley Hike
description: Otherworldly trail through towering phallic fairy chimneys and volcanic pillars, one of Cappadocia's most photographed landscapes.

name: Uchisar Castle
description: Towering volcanic rock fortress honeycombed with tunnels and rooms, offering the highest panoramic viewpoint in Cappadocia.

name: Kaymakli Underground City
description: Sprawling eight-level subterranean settlement with stables, churches, and wineries carved deep into soft tuff rock.

name: Avanos Pottery Workshop
description: Hands-on pottery class in the ceramics capital of Cappadocia, shaping red clay from the Kizilirmak River on a traditional wheel.

name: Cappadocia ATV Tour
description: Adrenaline-fuelled quad-bike ride through dusty valleys, fairy chimney trails, and sunset viewpoints across the lunar landscape.

name: Turkish Night Show
description: Lively evening of folk dancing, belly dancing, whirling dervishes, and unlimited local wine and dinner in a cave venue.

name: Ephesus Ancient City
description: Major Greco-Roman archaeological site featuring the Library of Celsus, Great Theatre, and the Temple of Artemis site.

name: House of the Virgin Mary
description: Sacred hilltop stone house traditionally held to be the final home of the Virgin Mary, a major Christian pilgrimage site.

name: Sirince Village
description: Charming hillside Greek village near Ephesus known for fruit wines, stone houses, and panoramic Aegean valley views.

name: Temple of Artemis Remains
description: Ruins of one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World — a single restored column marks where the colossal temple once stood.

name: Basilica of St. John
description: 6th-century Justinian basilica on Ayasuluk Hill believed to be built over the tomb of the Apostle John, with sweeping valley views.

name: Ephesus Museum Selcuk
description: Compact museum displaying stunning artefacts excavated from Ephesus including the ivory frieze, Artemis statues, and Roman coins.

name: Kusadasi Ladies Beach
description: Popular golden sand beach on the Aegean coast near Ephesus with calm turquoise waters, beach clubs, and waterfront restaurants.

name: Dilek Peninsula National Park
description: Pristine coastal park with secluded beaches, hiking trails through pine forests, and rare wildlife including wild horses.

name: Isa Bey Mosque
description: Elegant 14th-century Seljuk mosque with ornate marble courtyard and Corinthian column spolia reused from ancient Ephesus.

name: Traditional Turkish Bath in Selcuk
description: Authentic hamam experience in the town of Selcuk with marble steam rooms, scrub, and foam massage after a day exploring ruins.

name: Pamukkale Travertines
description: Surreal white calcium carbonate terraces filled with thermal mineral pools, a UNESCO World Heritage landscape.

name: Hierapolis Ancient City
description: Greco-Roman spa city atop Pamukkale with a vast necropolis, Roman theatre, and Cleopatra's antique pool.

name: Cleopatra's Antique Pool
description: Thermal pool with ancient Roman columns submerged in warm calcium-rich waters where legend says Cleopatra once bathed.

name: Kaklik Cave (Underground Pamukkale)
description: Hidden underground cavern with miniature travertine formations and thermal pools, often called the underground Pamukkale.

name: Laodikeia Ancient City
description: Impressive Roman city ruins just 6 km from Pamukkale, one of the Seven Churches of Revelation with ongoing excavations.

name: Pamukkale Tandem Paragliding
description: Soar above the white travertine terraces and Hierapolis ruins on a tandem paragliding flight with stunning aerial views.

name: Traditional Turkish Breakfast Farm
description: Lavish village breakfast spread on a family farm with fresh cheese, honey, jams, pastries, and tea among olive groves.

name: Karahayit Red Springs
description: Iron-rich thermal springs producing striking red-orange travertine terraces, with public and hotel thermal pools nearby.

name: Local Carpet Weaving Workshop
description: Visit a traditional carpet cooperative to watch artisans hand-weave silk and wool kilims on century-old looms.

name: Kaleici Old Town
description: Historic Roman harbour district with winding Ottoman lanes, Byzantine walls, and vibrant waterfront restaurants.

name: Duden Waterfalls
description: Twin waterfalls on the Antalya coast — the spectacular Lower Duden falls directly into the Mediterranean Sea.

name: Tunektepe Cable Car
description: Gondola ride to 618 m summit with panoramic views over Antalya Bay, the Taurus Mountains, and the Mediterranean coast.

name: Antalya Archaeological Museum
description: One of Turkey's finest museums with artefacts spanning from the Stone Age to Byzantine era, including a hall of gods statues.

name: Aspendos Theatre
description: Best-preserved Roman theatre in the world, still hosting concerts with perfect acoustics for 15,000 spectators after 1,800 years.

name: Perge Ancient City
description: Sprawling Greco-Roman city with a colonnaded street, stadium, baths, and agora — less crowded than Ephesus but equally impressive.

name: Konyaalti Beach
description: Long pebble beach backed by the dramatic Beydaglari Mountains, with clear Mediterranean water and lively beach parks.

name: Antalya Aquarium
description: One of the world's largest tunnel aquariums with a 131-metre walk-through tank, snow room, and tropical reptile house.

name: Olympos & Chimaera Eternal Flames
description: Natural gas flames burning from the mountainside for millennia near the ancient Lycian city of Olympos, best seen after dark.

name: Side Temple of Apollo
description: Romantic 2nd-century marble temple columns standing at the tip of a Mediterranean peninsula, spectacular at sunset.

name: Bodrum Castle & Underwater Archaeology Museum
description: Medieval Crusader castle housing the world's leading museum of underwater archaeology, with ancient shipwreck displays.

name: Blue Voyage Gulet Cruise
description: Sail the Aegean on a traditional wooden gulet visiting hidden coves, Kara Ada hot springs, and crystal-clear bays.

name: Bodrum Windmills & Sunset Viewpoint
description: Seven historic windmills crowning the Bodrum peninsula hill with spectacular 360-degree views over the Aegean at sunset.

name: Myndos Gate
description: Restored ancient gate of Halicarnassus where Alexander the Great was repelled, one of the last remnants of the city walls.

name: Bodrum Amphitheatre
description: Well-preserved ancient Halicarnassus theatre seating 13,000 on a hillside with sweeping views over the bay and castle below.

name: Bitez Beach
description: Sheltered sandy bay popular for windsurfing and kitesurfing, lined with mandarin orchards and laid-back beach restaurants.

name: Gumbet Watersports
description: Buzzing beach resort bay offering jet-skiing, parasailing, banana boat rides, and scuba diving just west of Bodrum centre.

name: Turkish Meze Cooking Class
description: Hands-on culinary workshop learning to prepare Aegean meze dishes with local herbs, olive oil, and seafood in a village kitchen.

name: Bardakci Bay Spa & Beach
description: Secluded crescent bay with upscale beach clubs and spa retreats tucked between rocky headlands, perfect for a restful day.

name: Bodrum Marina Sunset Walk
description: Evening stroll along the yacht-filled marina past seafood restaurants and boutiques with the castle glowing in golden light.

name: Ulu Cami (Grand Mosque)
description: Magnificent 14th-century mosque with 20 domes and a large indoor fountain — the greatest example of early Ottoman architecture.

name: Green Mosque & Green Tomb
description: Exquisite early 15th-century Ottoman mosque with turquoise Iznik tile interior and the mausoleum of Sultan Mehmed I.

name: Koza Han (Silk Bazaar)
description: 15th-century caravanserai at the heart of the ancient Silk Road trade, still home to silk merchants and cocoon traders.

name: Uludag National Park & Cable Car
description: Turkey's premier ski resort in winter and lush highland park in summer, reached by one of the world's longest cable cars.

name: Cumalikizik UNESCO Village
description: Beautifully preserved 700-year-old Ottoman village with colourful timber houses, cobblestone lanes, and famous gozleme breakfasts.

name: Iskender Kebab at the Original Restaurant
description: Taste the legendary Iskender kebab at Kebapci Iskender, where the dish was invented in 1867 with tender lamb over bread and yoghurt.

name: Bursa City Museum
description: Interactive museum tracing Bursa's journey from Ottoman capital to modern city with dioramas, shadow puppet displays, and multimedia.

name: Irgandi Bridge Bazaar
description: Rare Ottoman bridge-market spanning a river gorge, lined with tiny artisan shops selling handicrafts, paintings, and souvenirs.

name: Oylat Thermal Spa
description: Secluded hot spring resort in a forested canyon near Inegol, with mineral-rich waters emerging at 40°C surrounded by nature.

name: Mount Uludag Nature Trail
description: Alpine hiking trail through beech and fir forests on Turkey's Olympus, with wildflower meadows and mountain lake viewpoints.

name: Anitkabir — Ataturk's Mausoleum
description: Grand monumental mausoleum of Mustafa Kemal Ataturk, founder of the Turkish Republic, set in a vast ceremonial complex.

name: Museum of Anatolian Civilizations
description: World-class museum in a restored 15th-century bazaar, showcasing Hittite, Phrygian, and Neolithic artefacts spanning 10,000 years.

name: Ankara Citadel (Hisar)
description: Roman and Byzantine hilltop fortress with cobblestone lanes, traditional wooden houses, and sweeping views over the capital.

name: Haci Bayram Mosque
description: Revered 15th-century mosque beside the ruins of the Roman Temple of Augustus, a spiritual heart of the capital city.

name: Kocatepe Mosque
description: Ankara's largest mosque with four soaring minarets and an elegant neo-Ottoman interior, a modern landmark visible across the city.

name: Atakule Tower
description: 125-metre observation and communications tower with a revolving restaurant offering panoramic views over the Ankara skyline.

name: Genclik Park
description: Central green oasis with a large ornamental lake, amusement rides, tea gardens, and shaded walking paths near Ulus Square.

name: Ethnography Museum of Ankara
description: Elegant marble museum displaying Ottoman-era costumes, calligraphy, woodwork, and Seljuk carpets in Ataturk's former temporary tomb.

name: CerModern Art Gallery
description: Contemporary art centre in a converted railway depot hosting rotating exhibitions, sculpture gardens, and live performances.

name: Beypazari Ottoman Town
description: Picturesque day-trip town with restored Ottoman mansions, silver jewellery workshops, and famous dried-fruit stalls and carrot lokum.

name: Sumela Monastery
description: Dramatic 4th-century Greek Orthodox monastery carved into a sheer cliff face at 1,200 m altitude in the Pontic Mountains.

name: Uzungol Alpine Lake
description: Scenic highland lake surrounded by mist-covered tea plantations, forested mountains, and traditional Black Sea villages.

name: Hagia Sophia of Trabzon
description: 13th-century Byzantine church converted to mosque, adorned with remarkable frescoes and perched above the Black Sea coast.

name: Boztepe Hill Viewpoint
description: Panoramic hilltop park overlooking Trabzon city and the Black Sea, beloved by locals for tea gardens and romantic sunsets.

name: Caykur Tea Factory Tour
description: Guided tour of a working Black Sea tea processing plant, from fresh leaf rolling to tasting fragrant Turkish cay on terraced gardens.

name: Ataturk Pavilion Trabzon
description: Elegant early 20th-century mansion where Ataturk stayed, set in manicured gardens above the city with period furnishings intact.

name: Trabzon Bedesten Bazaar
description: Historic covered market in the old town selling local honey, butter, hazelnuts, copperware, and Black Sea handicrafts.

name: Ayder Plateau Hot Springs
description: Alpine plateau at 1,350 m with natural hot springs, misty waterfalls, and traditional yayla wooden houses in the Kackar foothills.

name: Firtina Valley Rafting
description: Thrilling whitewater rafting on the Storm River through lush green valleys, Ottoman stone bridges, and dense tea country.

name: Mevlana Museum
description: Spiritual heart of Turkey — the resting place of the great Sufi mystic Rumi, housed under the iconic fluted green turquoise dome.

name: Sema Whirling Dervish Ceremony
description: Hypnotic Mevlevi Sema ceremony — UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage — where dervishes spin in meditative prayer ritual.

name: Alaeddin Hill & Mosque
description: Ancient Seljuk citadel hill at the city centre topped with the 13th-century Alaeddin Mosque, Konya's oldest and grandest.

name: Karatay Madrasa Tile Museum
description: 13th-century Seljuk theological school now housing a dazzling collection of turquoise and cobalt Seljuk tiles under a soaring dome.

name: Sille Village
description: Ancient Greek-Ottoman village on the outskirts of Konya with a Byzantine rock church, a reservoir lake, and artisan workshops.

name: Konya Tropical Butterfly Garden
description: Indoor tropical garden housing hundreds of exotic butterfly species, parrots, and lush vegetation — a family-friendly oasis.

name: Traditional Etli Ekmek Tasting
description: Sample Konya's signature metre-long flatbread topped with spiced minced lamb, baked in a traditional stone oven at a local eatery.

name: Catalhoyuk Archaeological Site
description: UNESCO World Heritage Neolithic settlement dating to 7500 BC — one of the oldest known towns on Earth with excavated dwellings.

name: Kyoto Japanese Garden Konya
description: Tranquil Japanese-style garden donated by Konya's sister city Kyoto, with a koi pond, pagoda, and cherry blossoms in spring.

name: Troy Ancient City
description: Legendary Bronze Age site of Homer's Iliad, with 4,000 years of layered ruins and the iconic wooden horse replica.

name: Gallipoli Battlefields & Memorials
description: Moving WWI memorial sites including Anzac Cove, Chunuk Bair, and the Turkish monument — a pilgrimage for Australians and New Zealanders.

name: Canakkale Waterfront & Naval Museum
description: Lively Dardanelles strait waterfront with cafes, the replica minelayer Nusret, and a naval museum chronicling the strait's history.

name: Assos Ancient Temple of Athena
description: Doric temple ruins perched on a volcanic hilltop with sweeping views over the Aegean to Lesbos island, surrounded by olive groves.

name: Bozcaada Island Wine Tour
description: Ferry to Turkey's wine island for tastings at boutique vineyards, strolling the castle town, and swimming on sandy beaches.

name: Aynali Carsi Bazaar
description: Charming 19th-century mirrored bazaar in the city centre selling local ceramics, Canakkale pottery, spices, and sweets.

name: Canakkale Pottery Workshop
description: Try your hand at the region's famous Canakkale ceramics tradition, shaping and painting clay in a working studio.

name: Kilitbahir Castle
description: Imposing 15th-century Ottoman fortress shaped like a clover leaf, guarding the narrowest point of the Dardanelles across from Canakkale.

name: Dardanelles Open Water Swimming
description: Swim across the legendary Hellespont strait from Europe to Asia, retracing Lord Byron's 1810 crossing on an organised event.
